summary: Typosquat of @solana-labs/spl-token with install-time host reconnaissance and exfiltration
details: Package name @solana-labs/spl-toke is a one-character truncation of the legitimate @solana-labs/spl-token. The package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that runs on every default `npm install`. install.js collects host identifiers (hostname appears in multiple read sites at lines 10, 25, and 27) and posts the data outbound (POST at line 46), with a curl-based execution path at line 103. The combination of registry-name impersonation of a widely-used Solana scope, an automatic install-time hook, host-info collection, and outbound POST/curl from that hook matches a credential/host-recon supply-chain attack rather than any legitimate package behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
